frameworks.name: multicomponent working memory model
frameworks.one_line: four interacting components fractionate short-term storage and attentional control

frameworks.name: phonological loop
frameworks.one_line: modality-specific verbal-acoustic store maintained by subvocal rehearsal

frameworks.name: episodic buffer
frameworks.one_line: multimodal limited-capacity store binding subsystems with long-term memory

core_question: How can working memory be fractionated into interacting components that account for storage, attention, and binding across modalities?
key_assumptions: working memory is decomposable into separable subsystems
central executive has limited attentional resources
episodic buffer is passive and accessible to conscious awareness
key_findings: framework comprises four interacting components: phonological loop, sketchpad, central executive, episodic buffer
focused attention ('refreshing') can maintain information in both modality-specific stores
phonological loop additionally supports subvocal or overt rehearsal of verbalizable material
episodic buffer holds roughly four episodic chunks and integrates perception with long-term memory
interpretations: the multicomponent architecture supports both detailed fractionation and applications beyond the laboratory
central executive coordinates subsystems and selects perceptual information
